lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Excuse me. I am working on that statue at the moment. Please don't touch it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You are? But you're just sitting there.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Yes. I'm on a break.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I see. When does your break finish?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: When I decide to start work again. Right now, I'm enjoying sitting on this bench.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Yes? Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Well, now. Do you always go around asking about people like that? It's very rude, you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry! I didn't mean to pry!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: That's alright. My name is Louisiana Jones, although most people call me Anna. I'm an archaeologist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Do you work for the Varrock Museum?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Hah! No. I used to, but now I prefer to work freelance for independent employers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: I'm investigating something for someone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't really explain anything!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: I never said it would.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you investigating?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: I'm afraid I can't say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so who are you investigating it for?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: I'm afraid I can't say that either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so is there anything you CAN say?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: I can say that I can't say anything about what you want me to say!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I think you've said enough!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who does this statue represent?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: That, my dear, is the statue of the great god Saradomin himself. Stand and admire in awe, for you are in the presence of greatness!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...Yay Zamorak...!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: WHAT?!?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh...I said I've lost my anorak!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: I see. I'm afraid I haven't seen it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'd better go.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Okay, then!

sections.title: After completing What Lies Below, while the statue is intact

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Ah. You must be [player name], right? I have a bronze pickaxe here for you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks very much!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: My employer, Surok Magis, sent word to me that you may come to use the tunnel. You will need something to help you get in there. The pickaxe I've given to you should help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, thanks.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Okay, then. The tunnel awaits...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What tunnel?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Why, the Chaos Tunnel of course! I imagine Surok will have told you of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I see. Well, what can you tell me about it?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Well, story and rumour has it that the Dagon'hai built a tunnel here under the statue of Saradomin that would allow them to visit the Chaos Altar without having to go through the Wilderness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's extremely handy!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Yes, it is. Or, at least, it would be. Unfortunately, I haven't been able to get in there so far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: The tunnel entrance needs to be cleared before anyone can get in there. However, my Mining skill isn't high enough at all to do it myself. Perhaps you could have a go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, who are you?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: My name is Louisiana Jones, although most people call me Anna. I'm an archaeologist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pleased to meet you.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Likewise!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who does this statue represent?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: That, my dear, is the statue of the great god Saradomin himself. Stand and admire in awe for you are in the presence of greatness!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Consider me awed!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Indeed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Well story and rumour has it that the Dagon'hai built a tunnel under this statue of Saradomin that would allow them to visit the Chaos Altar without having to go through the Wilderness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's extremely handy!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Yes, it is. Or, at least, it would be. I came to excavate the tunnel but, unfortunately, I haven't been able to get in there so far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: The tunnel entrance needs to be cleared before anyone can get in there. However, my Mining skill just isn't what it used to be. Perhaps you could have a go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe. I might do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'd better go.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: Okay, then!

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: You did it! Oh, well done! How exciting!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, well, I better see what's down there, then.

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: You opened the tunnel; well done!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you going to go inside?

lines.speaker: Anna Jones
lines.text: I'd better not. My orders were to open up the entrance and wait for Surok to arrive. When he gets here, we'll go in together. He promised me a lot of gold for this job!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, then. I'm going in to have a look!
